issues.title: Release v1.21
issues.body:
# Timeline of a Knative release

We release each repository of `knative.dev` roughly every 3 months. Please check the [release schedule](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/README.md#release-schedule) to calculate when to start work on each checkpoint of a release timeline.

## Repos to be released

- All repos to be released are listed in this document
- Each repo indicates its releasability and other statuses, when applicable
- Each repo needs to be successfully updated to use the latest version of all dependencies **before** cutting its release branch

### T-minus 30 days

- [x] Release leads have been updated for the upcoming release (see [procedure](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#permissions-for-release-leads))
  - See workflow to check rollout: [![Owner Workflow](https://github.com/knative/release/actions/workflows/check-owners.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/knative/release/actions/workflows/check-owners.yaml)

### T-minus 14 days

- [x] Update release version in the following workflows

  - [x] Releasability (see: [procedure](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#updating-the-releasability-defaults))
  - [x] Check if Dependency Updates are working and the Knobots update-deps job has been bumped to the next release version (see: [procedure](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#bump-dependencies-in-auto-update-job))

- [x] An announcement has been made in the [**#knative**](https://app.slack.com/client/T08PSQ7BQ/C04LGHDR9K7) Slack channel that [`knative.dev/pkg`](https://github.com/knative/pkg) will be released in a week

### T-minus 7 days - releasing supporting repos

Guidance on [releasing a repository](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#releasing-a-repository).

- [x] An announcement has been made in the [**#knative**](https://app.slack.com/client/T08PSQ7BQ/C04LGHDR9K7) Slack channel that the cut of [`knative.dev/pkg`](https://github.com/knative/pkg) is imminent
- [x] Check the status of the nightly builds. See the badge against each core repo in Phase 1. Let the [working group leads](https://github.com/knative/community/blob/main/working-groups/WORKING-GROUPS.md) know about failures and ask them to investigate.Repeat this process throughout the week. This should not be a blocker to start cutting the supporting repositories mentioned below.

Notes

- Some of the supporting repos can be cut in parallel. Refer [Aligning dependencies](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md/#Aligning-dependencies).
- The release branch for each supporting repos must be done only after the branch for their dependencies is cut.

#### Cut Supporting Repositories _(Cut these in descending order (top to bottom) if unsure)_

- [knative/hack](https://github.com/knative/hack)
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative/pkg](https://github.com/knative/pkg)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][pkg-release-badge]][pkg-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative/networking](https://github.com/knative/networking)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][networking-release-badge]][networking-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative/caching](https://github.com/knative/caching)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][caching-release-badge]][caching-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative-extensions/reconciler-test](https://github.com/knative-extensions/reconciler-test)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][reconciler-test-release-badge]][reconciler-test-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut

### T-minus 1 day

- [x] Obtained ACK from each [working group lead](https://github.com/knative/community/blob/main/working-groups/WORKING-GROUPS.md) that the release is imminent and is green-lighted.

- [x] Re-check for status of the nightly releases and notify [working group leads](https://github.com/knative/community/blob/main/working-groups/WORKING-GROUPS.md) of failures. Repeat until all are passing.

### Release Day(s)

- If no acknowledgement is received from any of the WGL, send another message on slack and start working on the release after sometime.
- Review the [PROCEDURE](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#releasing-a-repository) on how to release a repo.
  Notes
- It is not required that all repos in the included sub-sections be released on the same day the release is scheduled to start.
- The release notes of a dependency are not a blocker for proceeding to cut a release for a repo.
- The prow job web page is very slow to respond and you need to click only once to rerun the job.
- Some repos can be released in parallel.Refer [Aligning dependencies](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#Aligning-dependencies).

#### Phase 0 - Core-Required Repositories _(Can be started after `knative/hack` branch has been cut)_

- [knative-extensions/eventing-integrations](https://github.com/knative-extensions/eventing-integrations)
  - [x] Nightly Job [![Nightly][eventing-integrations-nightly-badge]][eventing-integrations-nightly-page]
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][eventing-integrations-release-badge]][eventing-integrations-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
  - [x] Release
    - Prow Release [![Releases][eventing-integrations-prow-badge]][eventing-integrations-prow-job]
    - Latest Version [![Releases][eventing-integrations-version-badge]][eventing-integrations-release-page]
  - [x] Release Notes ([run workflow][release-note-workflow])

#### Phase 1 - Core Repositories _(Can be started after all the supporting and core-required repos have been successfully released)_

issues.title: Release v1.20
issues.body:
# Timeline of a Knative release

We release each repository of `knative.dev` roughly every 3 months. Please check the [release schedule](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/README.md#release-schedule) to calculate when to start work on each checkpoint of a release timeline.

## Repos to be released

- All repos to be released are listed in this document
- Each repo indicates its releasability and other statuses, when applicable
- Each repo needs to be successfully updated to use the latest version of all dependencies **before** cutting its release branch

### T-minus 30 days

- [x] Release leads have been updated for the upcoming release (see [procedure](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#permissions-for-release-leads))
  - See workflow to check rollout: [![Owner Workflow](https://github.com/knative/release/actions/workflows/check-owners.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/knative/release/actions/workflows/check-owners.yaml)

### T-minus 14 days

- [x] Update release version in the following workflows

  - [x] Releasability (see: [procedure](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#updating-the-releasability-defaults))
  - [x] Check if Dependency Updates are working and the Knobots update-deps job has been bumped to the next release version (see: [procedure](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#bump-dependencies-in-auto-update-job))

- [x] An announcement has been made in the [**#knative**](https://app.slack.com/client/T08PSQ7BQ/C04LGHDR9K7) Slack channel that [`knative.dev/pkg`](https://github.com/knative/pkg) will be released in a week

### T-minus 7 days - releasing supporting repos

Guidance on [releasing a repository](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#releasing-a-repository).

- [x] An announcement has been made in the [**#knative**](https://app.slack.com/client/T08PSQ7BQ/C04LGHDR9K7) Slack channel that the cut of [`knative.dev/pkg`](https://github.com/knative/pkg) is imminent
- [x] Check the status of the nightly builds. See the badge against each core repo in Phase 1. Let the [working group leads](https://github.com/knative/community/blob/main/working-groups/WORKING-GROUPS.md) know about failures and ask them to investigate.Repeat this process throughout the week. This should not be a blocker to start cutting the supporting repositories mentioned below.

Notes

- Some of the supporting repos can be cut in parallel. Refer [Aligning dependencies](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md/#Aligning-dependencies).
- The release branch for each supporting repos must be done only after the branch for their dependencies is cut.

#### Cut Supporting Repositories _(Cut these in descending order (top to bottom) if unsure)_

- [knative/hack](https://github.com/knative/hack)
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative/pkg](https://github.com/knative/pkg)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][pkg-release-badge]][pkg-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative/networking](https://github.com/knative/networking)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][networking-release-badge]][networking-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative/caching](https://github.com/knative/caching)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][caching-release-badge]][caching-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative-extensions/reconciler-test](https://github.com/knative-extensions/reconciler-test)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][reconciler-test-release-badge]][reconciler-test-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut

### T-minus 1 day

- [x] Obtained ACK from each [working group lead](https://github.com/knative/community/blob/main/working-groups/WORKING-GROUPS.md) that the release is imminent and is green-lighted.

- [x] Re-check for status of the nightly releases and notify [working group leads](https://github.com/knative/community/blob/main/working-groups/WORKING-GROUPS.md) of failures. Repeat until all are passing.

### Release Day(s)

- If no acknowledgement is received from any of the WGL, send another message on slack and start working on the release after sometime.
- Review the [PROCEDURE](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#releasing-a-repository) on how to release a repo.
  Notes
- It is not required that all repos in the included sub-sections be released on the same day the release is scheduled to start.
- The release notes of a dependency are not a blocker for proceeding to cut a release for a repo.
- The prow job web page is very slow to respond and you need to click only once to rerun the job.
- Some repos can be released in parallel.Refer [Aligning dependencies](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#Aligning-dependencies).

#### Phase 0 - Core-Required Repositories _(Can be started after `knative/hack` branch has been cut)_ PS: currently isnt part of update-deps matrix, manual update is required

- [knative-extensions/eventing-integrations](https://github.com/knative-extensions/eventing-integrations)
  - [x] Nightly Job [![Nightly][eventing-integrations-nightly-badge]][eventing-integrations-nightly-page]
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][eventing-integrations-release-badge]][eventing-integrations-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
  - [x] Release
    - Prow Release [![Releases][eventing-integrations-prow-badge]][eventing-integrations-prow-job]
    - Latest Version [![Releases][eventing-integrations-version-badge]][eventing-integrations-release-page]
  - [x] Release Notes ([run workflow][release-note-workflow])

#### Phase 1 - Core Repositories _(Can be started after all the supporting and core-required repos have been successfully released)_

- [knative/serving](https://github.com/knative/serving)
  - [x] Nightly Job [![Nightly][serving-nightly-badge]][serving-nightly-page]
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][serving-release-badge]][serving-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
  - [x] Release

    - Latest Version [![Releases][serving-version-badge]][serving-release-page]
  - [x] Release Notes ([run workflow][release-note-workflow])
- [knative/eventing](https://github.com/knative/eventing)

  - [x] Branch Cut

    - Prow Release Job [![Prow][eventing-prow-badge]][eventing-prow-job]
    - Latest Version [![Releases][eventing-version-badge]][eventing-release-page]
  - [x] Release Notes ([run workflow][release-note-workflow])
- [knative-extensions/net-contour](https://github.com/knative-extensions/net-contour)
  - [x] Nightly Job [![Nightly][net-contour-nightly-badge]][net-contour-nightly-page]
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][net-contour-release-badge]][net-contour-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
  - [x] Release
    - Prow Release [![Releases][net-contour-prow-badge]][net-contour-prow-job]
    - Latest Version [![Releases][net-contour-version-badge]][net-contour-release-page]
  - [x] Release Notes ([run workflow][release-note-workflow])
- [knative-extensions/net-gateway-api](https://github.com/knative-extensions/net-gateway-api)

issues.title: Release v1.19
issues.body:
# Timeline of a Knative release

We release each repository of `knative.dev` roughly every 3 months. Please check the [release schedule](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/README.md#release-schedule) to calculate when to start work on each checkpoint of a release timeline.

## Repos to be released

- All repos to be released are listed in this document
- Each repo indicates its releasability and other statuses, when applicable
- Each repo needs to be successfully updated to use the latest version of all dependencies **before** cutting its release branch

### T-minus 30 days

- [x] Release leads have been updated for the upcoming release (see [procedure](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#permissions-for-release-leads))
  - See workflow to check rollout: [![Owner Workflow](https://github.com/knative/release/actions/workflows/check-owners.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/knative/release/actions/workflows/check-owners.yaml)

### T-minus 14 days

- [x] Update release version in the following workflows

  - [x] Releasability (see: [procedure](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#updating-the-releasability-defaults))
  - [x] Check if Dependency Updates are working and the Knobots update-deps job has been bumped to the next release version (see: [procedure](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#bump-dependencies-in-auto-update-job))

- [x] An announcement has been made in the [**#knative**](https://app.slack.com/client/T08PSQ7BQ/C04LGHDR9K7) Slack channel that [`knative.dev/pkg`](https://github.com/knative/pkg) will be released in a week

### T-minus 7 days - releasing supporting repos

Guidance on [releasing a repository](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#releasing-a-repository).

- [x] An announcement has been made in the [**#knative**](https://app.slack.com/client/T08PSQ7BQ/C04LGHDR9K7) Slack channel that the cut of [`knative.dev/pkg`](https://github.com/knative/pkg) is imminent
- [x] Check the status of the nightly builds. See the badge against each core repo in Phase 1. Let the [working group leads](https://github.com/knative/community/blob/main/working-groups/WORKING-GROUPS.md) know about failures and ask them to investigate.Repeat this process throughout the week. This should not be a blocker to start cutting the supporting repositories mentioned below.

Notes

- Some of the supporting repos can be cut in parallel. Refer [Aligning dependencies](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md/#Aligning-dependencies).
- The release branch for each supporting repos must be done only after the branch for their dependencies is cut.

#### Cut Supporting Repositories

- [knative/hack](https://github.com/knative/hack)
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative/pkg](https://github.com/knative/pkg)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][pkg-release-badge]][pkg-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative/networking](https://github.com/knative/networking)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][networking-release-badge]][networking-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative/caching](https://github.com/knative/caching)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][caching-release-badge]][caching-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative-extensions/reconciler-test](https://github.com/knative-extensions/reconciler-test)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][reconciler-test-release-badge]][reconciler-test-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut

### T-minus 1 day

- [x] Obtained ACK from each [working group lead](https://github.com/knative/community/blob/main/working-groups/WORKING-GROUPS.md) that the release is imminent and is green-lighted.

- [x] Re-check for status of the nightly releases and notify [working group leads](https://github.com/knative/community/blob/main/working-groups/WORKING-GROUPS.md) of failures. Repeat until all are passing.

### Release Day(s)

- If no acknowledgement is received from any of the WGL, send another message on slack and start working on the release after sometime.
- Review the [PROCEDURE](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#releasing-a-repository) on how to release a repo.
  Notes
- It is not required that all repos in the included sub-sections be released on the same day the release is scheduled to start.
- The release notes of a dependency are not a blocker for proceeding to cut a release for a repo.
- The prow job web page is very slow to respond and you need to click only once to rerun the job.
- Some repos can be released in parallel.Refer [Aligning dependencies](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#Aligning-dependencies).

issues.title: Release v1.18
issues.body:
# Timeline of a Knative release

We release each repository of `knative.dev` roughly every 3 months. Please check the [release schedule](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/README.md#release-schedule) to calculate when to start work on each checkpoint of a release timeline.

## Repos to be released

- All repos to be released are listed in this document
- Each repo indicates its releasability and other statuses, when applicable
- Each repo needs to be successfully updated to use the latest version of all dependencies **before** cutting its release branch

### T-minus 30 days

- [x] Release leads have been updated for the upcoming release (see [procedure](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#permissions-for-release-leads))
  - See workflow to check rollout: [![Owner Workflow](https://github.com/knative/release/actions/workflows/check-owners.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/knative/release/actions/workflows/check-owners.yaml)

### T-minus 14 days

- [x] Update release version in the following workflows

  - [x] Releasability (see: [procedure](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#updating-the-releasability-defaults))
  - [x] Check if Dependency Updates are working and the Knobots update-deps job has been bumped to the next release version (see: [procedure](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#bump-dependencies-in-auto-update-job))

- [x] An announcement has been made in the [**#knative**](https://app.slack.com/client/T08PSQ7BQ/C04LGHDR9K7) Slack channel that [`knative.dev/pkg`](https://github.com/knative/pkg) will be released in a week

### T-minus 7 days - releasing supporting repos

Guidance on [releasing a repository](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#releasing-a-repository).

- [x] An announcement has been made in the [**#knative**](https://app.slack.com/client/T08PSQ7BQ/C04LGHDR9K7) Slack channel that the cut of [`knative.dev/pkg`](https://github.com/knative/pkg) is imminent
- [x] Check the status of the nightly builds. See the badge against each core repo in Phase 1. Let the [working group leads](https://github.com/knative/community/blob/main/working-groups/WORKING-GROUPS.md) know about failures and ask them to investigate.Repeat this process throughout the week. This should not be a blocker to start cutting the supporting repositories mentioned below.

Notes

- Some of the supporting repos can be cut in parallel. Refer [Aligning dependencies](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md/#Aligning-dependencies).
- The release branch for each supporting repos must be done only after the branch for their dependencies is cut.

#### Cut Supporting Repositories

- [knative/hack](https://github.com/knative/hack)
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative/pkg](https://github.com/knative/pkg)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][pkg-release-badge]][pkg-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative/networking](https://github.com/knative/networking)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][networking-release-badge]][networking-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative/caching](https://github.com/knative/caching)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][caching-release-badge]][caching-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative-extensions/reconciler-test](https://github.com/knative-extensions/reconciler-test)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][reconciler-test-release-badge]][reconciler-test-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut

### T-minus 1 day

- [x] Obtained ACK from each [working group lead](https://github.com/knative/community/blob/main/working-groups/WORKING-GROUPS.md) that the release is imminent and is green-lighted.

- [x] Re-check for status of the nightly releases and notify [working group leads](https://github.com/knative/community/blob/main/working-groups/WORKING-GROUPS.md) of failures. Repeat until all are passing.

### Release Day(s)

- If no acknowledgement is received from any of the WGL, send another message on slack and start working on the release after sometime.
- Review the [PROCEDURE](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#releasing-a-repository) on how to release a repo.
  Notes
- It is not required that all repos in the included sub-sections be released on the same day the release is scheduled to start.
- The release notes of a dependency are not a blocker for proceeding to cut a release for a repo.
- The prow job web page is very slow to respond and you need to click only once to rerun the job.
- Some repos can be released in parallel.Refer [Aligning dependencies](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#Aligning-dependencies).

issues.title: Add eventing-integrations repo to the release train
issues.body:
There is currently no released artifact for the integration images.

Ideally the release produces as ConfigMap with the tagged/sha'ed images.  

The repo should be released before the eventing-core, since the ConfigMap artifact needs to be injected into eventing-core repo, as a dependency

issues.title: Release v1.17
issues.body:
# Timeline of a Knative release

We release each repository of `knative.dev` roughly every 3 months. Please check the [release schedule](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/README.md#release-schedule) to calculate when to start work on each checkpoint of a release timeline.

## Repos to be released

- All repos to be released are listed in this document
- Each repo indicates its releasability and other statuses, when applicable
- Each repo needs to be successfully updated to use the latest version of all dependencies **before** cutting its release branch

### T-minus 30 days

- [x] Release leads have been updated for the upcoming release (see [procedure](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#permissions-for-release-leads))
  - See workflow to check rollout: [![Owner Workflow](https://github.com/knative/release/actions/workflows/check-owners.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/knative/release/actions/workflows/check-owners.yaml)

### T-minus 14 days

- [x] Update release version in the following workflows

  - [x] Releasability (see: [procedure](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#updating-the-releasability-defaults))
  - [x] Check if Dependency Updates are working and the Knobots update-deps job has been bumped to the next release version (see: [procedure](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#bump-dependencies-in-auto-update-job))

- [x] An announcement has been made in the [**#knative**](https://app.slack.com/client/T08PSQ7BQ/C04LGHDR9K7) Slack channel that [`knative.dev/pkg`](https://github.com/knative/pkg) will be released in a week

### T-minus 7 days - releasing supporting repos

Guidance on [releasing a repository](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#releasing-a-repository).

- [x] An announcement has been made in the [**#knative**](https://app.slack.com/client/T08PSQ7BQ/C04LGHDR9K7) Slack channel that the cut of [`knative.dev/pkg`](https://github.com/knative/pkg) is imminent
- [x] Check the status of the nightly builds. See the badge against each core repo in Phase 1. Let the [working group leads](https://github.com/knative/community/blob/main/working-groups/WORKING-GROUPS.md) know about failures and ask them to investigate.Repeat this process throughout the week. This should not be a blocker to start cutting the supporting repositories mentioned below.

Notes

- Some of the supporting repos can be cut in parallel. Refer [Aligning dependencies](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md/#Aligning-dependencies).
- The release branch for each supporting repos must be done only after the branch for their dependencies is cut.

#### Cut Supporting Repositories

- [knative/hack](https://github.com/knative/hack)
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative/pkg](https://github.com/knative/pkg)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][pkg-release-badge]][pkg-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative/networking](https://github.com/knative/networking)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][networking-release-badge]][networking-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative/caching](https://github.com/knative/caching)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][caching-release-badge]][caching-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut
- [knative-extensions/reconciler-test](https://github.com/knative-extensions/reconciler-test)
  - [x] Dependencies up to date - [![releasabilty][reconciler-test-release-badge]][reconciler-test-release-workflow]
  - [x] Branch Cut

### T-minus 1 day

- [x] Obtained ACK from each [working group lead](https://github.com/knative/community/blob/main/working-groups/WORKING-GROUPS.md) that the release is imminent and is green-lighted.

- [x] Re-check for status of the nightly releases and notify [working group leads](https://github.com/knative/community/blob/main/working-groups/WORKING-GROUPS.md) of failures. Repeat until all are passing.

### Release Day(s)

- If no acknowledgement is received from any of the WGL, send another message on slack and start working on the release after sometime.
- Review the [PROCEDURE](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#releasing-a-repository) on how to release a repo.
  Notes
- It is not required that all repos in the included sub-sections be released on the same day the release is scheduled to start.
- The release notes of a dependency are not a blocker for proceeding to cut a release for a repo.
- The prow job web page is very slow to respond and you need to click only once to rerun the job.
- Some repos can be released in parallel.Refer [Aligning dependencies](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/PROCEDURES.md#Aligning-dependencies).

issues.title: Investigate ways to validate dependencies are aligned
issues.body: A GHA should be created to check if the dependencies lined up properly for a nightly and if so promote that nightly to a release canditate (RC) or at least tag it as a RC. Then one would be able to cut a release from one of the RC saving us from going through the current manual process of aligning the dependencies.

issues.title: Document supported versions of Serving and Eventing for the Knative Operator in docs
issues.body: Per discussion in https://github.com/knative/docs/pull/5096/files#r922284883, the supported operator versions (https://github.com/knative/docs/blob/30eb3c949642496bca507f89e05c08bb4a977bc7/docs/install/operator/knative-with-operators.md?plain=1#L10) need to be updated in each release.

issues.title: Add instructions for how to put together a blog post for a new release
Update release README with clear steps for release notes
issues.body:
# Context
The [release steps](https://github.com/knative/release/blob/main/README.md) mention generating release notes and how this can be done. However, it would be valuable to have the expected actions more clearly documented

For example, run the release notes jobs on semantically versioned repo 

## Addtional context
This was missed in the v1.3 release with 3 first time release leads. 
- We didn’t run it for all repos
  - Wasn’t run for eventing-rabbitmq, eventing-kafka
  - Docs folks manually collected this and the release blog got incorrect info [link](https://knative.slack.com/archives/C9CV04DNJ/p1647988832774519)

## Ideal state
- running the release note generation process and adding it to a release is automated like the github release generation is.

issues.title: A diagram that shows the repos and dependencies would be helpful for understanding the relationship between repos
issues.body:
# Context
The Knative release process requires releasing repositories in a particular order (based on their dependency chain). It would be valuable to have a visual representation of the dependencies to help folks understand the relationships across repos.

## Additional context
@salaboy created [this](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1PEDCcEZWJ5BYccoaOTtiZDk-dssKrXZ3-wwH2S6yUzU/edit#heading=h.u10frnkwx141) which was useful but likely needs some tweaks.

issues.title: Figure out release shepherds roles and responsibilities
issues.body:
> Is it that only serving folks can release serving and eventing folks eventing ? 

I think the goal is to have folks who are familiar with an area participating in the release. ie. I wouldn't be the best person to make a call whether we should delay/halt the release of an eventing repo if the nightly releases were failing. 

> Actually I would prefer more a roster that is aligned along time zones. I.e. one roster for APAC/EMEA and one for NA, and everybody should be able to release everything with an handover EOD of the first group. Wdyt ?

If we add a step were the leads sign off and then folks in the roster just do the mechanics of the release I think basing things on timezone would work well

_Originally posted by @dprotaso in https://github.com/knative/release/issues/29#issuecomment-874176173_
